name: MongoDB Atlas — sharded cluster (calibration #8, benchANT YCSB caching benchmark)

descriptions.loaders: 8 YCSB CoreWorkload loader nodes (c5.4xlarge), each targeting 6,000 op/s (48,000 combined nominal), 50/50 read/update, uniform key distribution, ~1 KB records (10 fields x 100 bytes). Achieved combined 44,898.4 op/s (93.5% of the nominal target) — the loaders could not sustain the target rate, the signature of a server-side ceiling.
descriptions.mongo: One MongoDB Atlas 6.0 SHARDED cluster (6x M200_NVME, AWS us-west-2), modeled as a single aggregate SDA node — the same aggregate-cluster convention the Cassandra/Kafka cluster entries use. connectionPool (600) is PINNED: the MongoDB driver's documented default maxPoolSize (100 connections per client-to-shard mongos route — MONGO_POOL_SOURCE) x 6 shards. connectionHeldMs is the FITTED tunable (a NODE-scoped tunable, not shared with DeathStarBench's type-scoped db.mongodb:connectionHeldMs — a different, near-idle deployment): fitted by `pnpm calibrate` to 13.1014 ms, the value that reproduces the measured 13.8101 ms blended read/update mean at the achieved 44,898.4 op/s load, given the pinned pool (post-fit residual -0.9%, grid-search granularity — see CALIBRATION-REPORT.md). `throughput` (45,797 op/s) is a derived, non-fitted display value kept consistent with pool/held by construction (== 600 / (connectionHeldMs/1000)), the same consistency convention the Redis/Cassandra entries follow.
